labs.title: Beaconkeeper: Twenty Lanterns to Dawn
labs.summary: Night watch on a broken Ubuntu host. The Beacon service is dark and twenty things are wrong — diagnose and repair them with everyday Linux tools, and light every lantern before dawn.
labs.prompt: You've taken the night watch on harbor, an Ubuntu host whose Beacon service went dark after a sloppy maintenance shift — the dashboard is wrong or missing. Restore it to a healthy, supportable state using ordinary Linux tools: twenty concrete repairs spanning services, logs, permissions, nginx routing, releases, data, backups, and operator access. Log in as keeper — work right here in the browser shell or SSH in from your own terminal, your choice — and run ops status to see all twenty checkpoints; a short brief prints on login. Work from evidence on the host rather than guessing, and preserve useful data as you go. Use ops verify <n> to light each lantern as you repair it, and ops hint <n> when you want a nudge. Leave a concise operator handoff in /srv/beacon/RUNBOOK.md covering final state, health checks, deploy and rollback, logs, backups, and access. A few checkpoints want real rigor rather than surface fixes — logrotate must pass a dry run, and the new operator needs a restrictive umask. Light all twenty lanterns before dawn.
hints: The lab brief prints on login; ops brief and ops status list all 20 checkpoints—each line includes the objective text ops verify checks.
Work in phases: orient (chart, facts, cleanup) → services (API, worker, nginx) → deploy and data → handoff (backups, logrotate, operator, runbook).
Expect work across services, logs, permissions, HTTP/nginx routing, release data, backups, and operator access.
Use ops verify <n|all> only when local evidence supports the fix; finish with a complete /srv/beacon/RUNBOOK.md handoff.
Fill /srv/beacon/OPERATOR_FACTS with KEY=value lines matching the local chart: SERVICE_NAME, APP_ROOT, INTERNAL_PORT, PUBLIC_HEALTH_URL, DB_PATH, BACKUP_DIR.
